title: Definitions
intro: How every metric on this dashboard is defined, so the numbers mean the same thing to everyone. Marketing leads with leading indicators; revenue and win rate are the lagging scoreboard and are owned by RevOps.

terms.def: A contact flagged marketing_intent_tier = High. The earliest read on high-intent demand and our north-star metric. Counted by create date in the month.
A contact that entered the Marketing Qualified Lead lifecycle stage in the month.
A contact that entered the Sales Qualified Lead stage in the month, sales has accepted it as worth pursuing.
A contact that entered the Opportunity stage in the month.
SQL stage-entries divided by MQL stage-entries in the month. Directional, not a true cohort rate (a contact entering SQL this month may have become an MQL earlier).

Leading indicators (HIH, MQL, SQL, attribution) are what marketing controls and acts on. Lagging indicators (revenue, win rate) are sales outcomes that trail the work by roughly two quarters in K-12, shown as context only.
Sum of deal amount for non-test, non-RFP deals marked closed-won in the month, across the District and School pipelines. Includes $0 pilots (real business activity).
dealtype of New Business, Pilot, or Pilot Expansion. A pilot converting or expanding counts as new business.
dealtype of Renewal, Upsell, Add On, or Cross Sell (growth within an existing customer).
Count of non-test, non-RFP closed-won deals in the month, regardless of dollar amount (so pilots are visible).

sections.heading: Win rate (lagging, RevOps owns the official number)

terms.def: Won deals divided by won plus lost deals that reached a close stamp in the month. Swings with when sales clears stale deals, so read with care.
Won dollars divided by won plus lost dollars in the month. A $300 loss and a $150K loss are not the same, so the dollar view matters.
Won divided by all deals in the segment fiscal-year-to-date. This is the basis that matches RevOps. Gated off until the RevOps deal-inclusion definition is confirmed.
Internal test deals and competitive RFP deals are excluded from BOTH the won and lost sides.
When most of a month's losses are aged deals (created more than 90 days ago) cleared in a cleanup, the monthly win rate is distorted and is flagged.
Deal Segment = Single Site or Small District.
Deal Segment = Medium District.
Deal Segment = Large District.
Deal Segment = Enterprise District. Segment is company-level, so a single school inherits its parent district's size.
K-8 social studies curriculum (topic area: Social Studies). Our core, highest-volume product.
K-2 integrated ELA + social studies (topic area: ELA). A growth product.
Secondary world history curriculum.
Licensed Pre-K / TK early-childhood curriculum (topic area: ECE / Pre-K). A growth product.

Funnel-by-product uses the contact product_interest field (strong at top of funnel, partial down-funnel). Revenue-by-product uses the deal product_s_ field, which is multi-select, so product splits are directional and do not sum to totals.
Share of new contacts with a utm_campaign populated. Low coverage means source-level cuts are unreliable. Target is 30% or higher.
Keywords we track in Search Atlas, how many currently rank at all, and how many rank in positions 1 to 10.
Average Google position across ranked keywords (lower is better).
Keywords grouped by Brand, Social Studies, ELA, ECE / Pre-K, and Other, so we can see where we rank by product line. MoM deltas begin once a second month of history exists.
Oct 1 to Sep 30. Pipeline, targets, and the cumulative win rate are measured against it.

Districts buy on the school-year calendar, so deals concentrate in spring and summer (Apr to Aug) for the upcoming year. Revenue peaks May to Jul, offset from the Sep 30 close. New-lead volume builds fall to winter, peaks Jan to Feb, and is low in summer.
Funnel + revenue from HubSpot CRM, web metrics from GA4, organic search from Search Atlas. Backfilled history (Jun 2025 to May 2026) is not test-excluded except the current month.
The date stamped at the top reflects the last reporting run that wrote this data.
